reviews.review_msg: How stuents can take so many questions in the exam??? Just gave them around 1:30 hour for 65 MCQ??
This course felt like a waste of time and money. The slides showed all AI-generated and disorganized. She is not accessible and frequently redirected questions to TAs.
She even could not speak English well, but gave bunch of complicated hard questions to add so many difficulties for the students. What are you doing Ms??
What she gave the questions does not match what she taught in the class, worst prof ever met so far.
The hardest exam I ever taken, so many questions with bunch of long readings. She is not testing you about marketing, just for IELTS reading.
Everything is so disorganized, and no one could understand what she is talking about
Don't choose her course, stay away from her.
Very sweet lady, but her slides are so disorganized and visually unpleasant. Some slides are completely unreadable tbh. But its a pretty straightforward class.
Snooze fest. watched fifa instead
She is not suit to be a Prof, her accent could not understand by the whole class. Gave the questions to discussed are all from AI. Not recommed
Everything is so disorganized and she has not realized what she gave to the class is not useful to study with, stay away from her
So bad experience, could not understand what she taught in class

Fascinating subject, but poorly delivered. Lectures felt lamentable. Exams pulled straight from her slides. Group projects came with vague rubrics that left little clarity on expectations. Last-minute assignment changes were possible — things like adding an entire section the night before the deadline. Your experience depend heavily on your TA.

Slightly disorganized as assignment instructions are not clearly laid out. Some deadlines not explicitly stated. Course content was quite easy, little to no strictness. Midterm not difficult. Group projects are just the luck of the draw, however a bad group isn't entirely detrimental as grading was not that harsh. Fun enough overall
Lectures are dry and offer little value. Assignments are unclear with inconsistent rubrics. She includes uncited AI images in her slide yet holds students to a much higher standard.
Seems like she was not know what she was teaching, just read the slides without any actual learning contents. JUST RUN

Claudia is very kind and has a good sense of humour but the class itself is quite boring and quite frankly not very useful. Lectures are not worth going to, exams are just like 50 MCQs, personal assignments are pretty chill, group projects are marked hard and the guidelines weren't super clear and marking is highly variable depending on TA.

This course felt like a waste of time and money. The material covered was minimal, and the slides often appeared low-effort and possibly AI-generated. The professor was not very accessible and frequently redirected questions to TAs. Overall, the course quality did not meet expectations for SFU.
Nice professor, impossible accent. Group projects were difficult. Midterm and Final could be done by a child.
Very unorganized and incoherent during lectures. Assignments and multiple group projects, all with vague criteria and conflicting instructions. Avoid if possible.

Claudia is a great and understanding prof. She tries to make class engaging by having activities throughout the lecture. She is extremely friendly and accommodating. One of the best profs for marketing.
So disorganized, and PPT Slides are so easy but the exam gave so hard and "Choose the best answer", how students know which is the best answer in your heart? Avoid....
Very disorganized
VERY DISORGNIZED! I believed that she even did not know what she taught! AVOID PLEASE!

Lectures were pretty disorganized. Slides looked disgusting and the professor would just be reading word for word off them 90% of the time. In the most respectful way possible, she has a really abrasive Spanish accent. She spoke in an ear piercing pitch that irked me. Otherwise, the class was pretty straightforward and no curve balls were thrown.

INCREDIBLY DISORGANIZED! Everything she and the TA said would always contradict. Nothing she posted or uploaded was correct; she eventually just stopped uploading lectures and other things. Grading rubrics were not followed or even written correctly, and would change at the last minute. No clear instructions and lots of weekly work.
Love love love!! Cannot recommend enough! Take her 100%

Firstly, this review is for BUS 341 not 343, but that wasn't an option to choose. Professor Claudia was amazing! She was a fantastic lecturer, and she was very engaging. All the course material was tied to practical tasks and I found it relevant to my entrepreneurial journey. I left the course with better speaking skills, and a richer network.

Claudia is a very nice person, however this class is extremely unorganized. I had her for BUS341. The marking was extremely difficult with no clear rubric at all. The class felt extremely disorganized and sometimes we would have 3 assignments due in one week. It was alot of work for a Business minor course. Also, extremely group project heavy!

Such an amazing professor I loved her so much! I had her for BUS 341 not 343 but that wasn't an option to select on. She was incredibly kind and caring, explained concepts super well, and I found them super relevant to my business and daily life. I recommend her to anyone, she's incredible!
she has a tendancy to shout teach for some reason

Claudia's exams are on everything she talks about in class. People are giving her low stars because they don't show up to lectures. As long as you show up and participate, you'll do fine. Her grading criteria couldn't be more clearer and if you do bad in this course then you obviously didn't put in any effort.

Honestly, Claudia was a very sweet person; she would do her best to help students out and cared for them. However, the class was very disorganized; everyone would usually be confused at her teaching, and her accent made it even worse. The class has a heavy workload with vague grading criteria. There is a lot of group work and read the textbook!

First off, she seems really nice & I imagine she is a sweet lady.

However, I am paying around $1,000 for this class. She is extremely unorganized, confusing and very difficult to understand. I am not happy.

Yes the exams are easy, but she is totally incompetent.

She people may complain that Claudias lectures are hard to understand, possibly because she's ESL, but I thought Claudia was a very good prof. She gave a lecture dedicated to helping on the final and she watered down the final to help us get a better grade. She's good in my experience.

Prof was not great. Her Powerpoint slides had inaccuracies, she could not provide examples on the topics in class, grading criteria was vague, and fairly heavy workload. 1 individual presentation, 2 group presentations, and 1 group report. Upside is tests are easy, all MCQ. pray for a good group
Seems nice, lectures aren't too difficult to understand. Will edit and update once the semester ends.
